message:
fix(telemetry): broaden Histogram::record guard from NaN-only to all non-finite (ft-trot7)

`Histogram::record` previously guarded with `if value.is_nan() { return; }`
per ft-761tz (disk_pressure): skip NaN samples so one bad read cannot
permanently poison observability via `total_sum += NaN`.

That guard let `f64::INFINITY` and `f64::NEG_INFINITY` through — and
they are just as absorbing under the operations the histogram performs:

1. `total_sum += INF` → sum pinned to INF for every subsequent sample.
   Mean collapses to INF (or NaN once a -INF ever arrives).
2. `min = min.min(value)` / `max = max.max(value)` — the first non-
   finite sample pins min to -INF or max to +INF forever. The sample
   window rolls over but min/max do not.
3. `HistogramSummary` serialises through serde_json, which per RFC 8259
   cannot represent ±INF and emits JSON `null`. Downstream telemetry
   payloads then silently drop the value, so a single stray INF
   sample erases the histogram from dashboards until the process
   restarts.

Switch the guard to `if !value.is_finite() { return; }`, which rejects
NaN and ±INF in one check. Matches the fail-closed posture of ft-761tz
(which this comment block now cross-references in-line) and shares the
shape of ft-n4fdx (bayesian_ledger) and ft-c17iw (semantic_anomaly):
reject non-finite at the ingress so one bad sample cannot render the
histogram permanently broken.

Pinning tests (all added under the `-- ft-trot7 --` section):

- ft_trot7_histogram_record_pos_infinity_does_not_poison_mean:
  Inserts [10, 20, INF, 30], asserts mean == 20.0, count == 3,
  retained == 3. If a future regression drops the is_finite guard,
  total_sum goes to INF and this test fails loudly.
- ft_trot7_histogram_record_neg_infinity_does_not_poison_mean:
  Symmetric check for -INF.
- ft_trot7_histogram_all_infinity_keeps_mean_none: all-Inf stream
  must surface as "no samples" — mean None, count 0, min_max None.
  Matches the all-NaN contract established by the original ft-761tz
  test.
- ft_trot7_histogram_summary_max_stays_finite_after_inf_attempt:
  Pins summary().max to finite after an Inf attempt. Catches the
  specific downstream-dashboard regression where `summary.max` would
  serialise as JSON null.

No behaviour change for finite samples; the hot path is still a
single branch.
